title: Festuca expansa
intro: Festuca expansa, also known as buffalo grass, is a perennial grass species native to North America, valued for its drought tolerance and low maintenance requirements. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as the potential to aid in wound healing and reduce fever. The bioactive constituents of Festuca expansa include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Festuca expansa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support immune function and reduce inflammation in traditional medicine practices.
taxonomy: Festuca expansa is classified within the family Poaceae. This family, commonly known as the grass family, comprises various species of grasses. Within the Poaceae family, Festuca expansa is further classified in the genus Festuca, which is a group of temperate grasses. The genus Festuca is often divided into several subgenera, and Festuca expansa falls within one of these subgenera. However, the specific subgenus of Festuca expansa is not widely documented in available sources.
